headline: Police body camera pilot covers only 800 officers
context: After 3 years of planning, the Royal Malaysia Police body camera pilot launched in 2024 covering only 800 officers in Kuala Lumpur.

cards.big: Body cameras deployed to only 800 of 130,000 police officers after 3-year pilot
cards.sub: Less than 1% of force covered despite RM24 million procurement

cards.big: No legislation mandates body camera usage or governs footage retention
cards.sub: Officers have discretion on when to activate cameras without oversight

cards.big: IPCC recommended mandatory body cameras for all frontline officers in 2023
cards.sub: Accountability requires evidence preservation, not optional recording

cards.big: Community surveys show 78% support mandatory body cameras for police
cards.sub: Public confidence in police complaint mechanisms remains below 35%

cards.big: 800 cameras on 130,000 officers is a gesture, not a reform
Indonesia deployed body cameras to 15,000 officers within 18 months of pilot
